topic: Rewrite-verify of RW-002 (part6.html#p6-610-9 Steelman Evidence Ladder — Category G forward-reference previews)

RWR-1.finding: Section 6.10 is a section_details_block (no formal 'verdict' field). The implicit verdict — 'even the strongest tier (STEELMAN-01) fails the most basic test of a scientific prediction: out-of-sample accuracy on data that wasn't used in calibration' — is preserved verbatim. TLDR's three-part summary (non-discriminating predictions, fitted parameters, timestamp-wrong-side) preserved verbatim. The closing 'Self-Assessment as Insulation' meta-claim and the kill-shot framework critique paragraph are preserved verbatim.

RWR-2.finding: Load-bearing dome terminology preserved: STEELMAN-01..04 labels, V13 Finsler, Sydney-Perth 4,352 km, Indian Pacific railway, eclipse magnetic ensemble, Chapman (1933), Sq currents, Tesla 0.08484 s, 1.574c, US 787412, disc radius 20,015 km, lambda_g 8,619 km, Schumann/Polaris/parameterization triple, 28-scored / 39-domain / 61.46 sigma. None paraphrased away.

RWR-3.finding: Diff scan finds zero deletions and 4 insertions. All original connectives (however, but, despite, because, since, therefore, only when, only if, unless, even though) are preserved verbatim. The inserted 'X, which Y, for the full Z' construction is slightly heavier than the original 'X for the full Z' but the original 'See ... for the full ... analysis' wrapper is preserved exactly around each new clause.

RWR-4.reason: no-Category-D-restructure-applied (only Category G — forward-reference previews — inserted in-place; paragraph order, sentence order, and section ordering all preserved verbatim)

RWR-5.finding: No new factual claims, numbers, or citations introduced. The 4 inserted clauses (RWR-12 handles them in detail) all paraphrase existing tldrs in target sections 2.9, 6.2, and 4.1 — they are within-document cross-reference previews, not new content. Numbers that appear in the inserted clauses (170 meters, 28-scored, 39-domain, 61.46 sigma) all come from the target sections own tldrs, not from outside the documents prior content.

RWR-6.finding: Programmatic diff confirms zero deletions. All 7 paragraphs of body prose, all 5 subheadings (SHA-256 Recycling / STEELMAN-01..04 / The Pattern), and the 2-paragraph closing meta-analysis are present verbatim. Kernel-of-truth setups (the 'What's genuinely strong:' / 'Why it fails:' two-blocks structure inside STEELMAN-01..04) all preserved. Caveats preserved: V13 claims 8.4% via three new free parameters and unpublished n(r); three Kp escape clauses on the 2026 eclipse test; lambda_g fitted to three data points (Schumann ~9,500 km / Polaris 4,750 km / parameterization 9,086 km) with one degree of freedom; the 3-conflicting-prediction-values issue for EBR (-21.7 / -28.9 / -29.1 nT).

RWR-7.finding: Chapman (1933) — the only external citation in the surface — remains in the STEELMAN-02 paragraph attached to the same Sq-currents claim it originally supported. Internal cross-references (Part 5; Part 5 Test 1; Section 2.9 x2; Section 6.2; Section 6.4; Section 3.1b; Section 4.1) all preserved in their original anchor positions; 4 receive G-previews appended after the closing tag, none are relocated.

RWR-8.finding: CONTENT_PRESERVATION_AUDIT lists 75 preserved tokens, all confirmed present in rewritten_text. Numbers added by the previews (170 meters tied to 40,030 km / 2pi R_Earth in Section 6.2; 28-scored/39-domain/61.46 sigma tied to the Live Power panel audit in Section 4.1) are attached to the same physical quantities they describe in their respective source tldrs — no number-quantity drift.

RWR-9.finding: Predicted composite 0.4 -> 2.8 (delta +2.4, clears 1.5 minimum_improvement_delta gate). Length stays at floor 1 (rewritten 2338 plain words vs target_max 700 = 3.34x over; rewriter correctly predicted no length-axis change). Understandability predicted 0 -> 4 driven by forward_references recovery -10 -> -6 via +1 per G-substitution heuristic. The rewriter explicitly flags the dominant uncertainty in method_note: whether the scorer rescores fully when 4 of ~10 bare references are substituted. This is honest calibration uncertainty and Q-OP-7's recalibration audit will catch any systematic drift post-hoc. Per-RW plausibility check: the 4 G-substitutions are confirmed present and well-formed in rewritten_text (programmatic diff agrees with rewriter's count), so the +4 recovery accounting is mechanically correct.

RWR-12.finding: All 4 G-previews are paraphrases of their source section tldrs with load-bearing nouns preserved. Preview 1 (Section 2.9 occ 1, 'self-referential because its input coordinates are derived from globe distances'): nouns 'V13 Finsler' / 'input coordinates' / 'globe distances' verbatim in source; 'self-referential' = synonym for source's 'circular'; directional claim preserved. Preview 2 (Section 2.9 occ 2, 'circular conversion of globe positions into dome coordinates'): nouns verbatim from source tldr. Preview 3 (Section 6.2, '40,030 km disc diameter equals 2pi x R_Earth to within 170 meters because the disc radius is really a spherical arc length'): every load-bearing noun verbatim from source. Preview 4 (Section 4.1, 'audits the dome's 28-scored / 39-domain / 61.46 sigma Fisher-combined dashboard and shows the count keeps growing without any new physics'): all four numerical anchors and the 'count keeps growing without any new physics' phrase verbatim from source tldr. Zero new framings introduced; zero re-attribution; hedging matches the source-tldr level in all four cases.

RWR-13.reason: no-Category-H-applied (no external-link previews in this rewrite; Chapman (1933) external link in STEELMAN-02 was preserved verbatim, not previewed)

curmudgeon_open_concerns: Two minor operator-attention observations, neither blocking: (1) The discipline_gap_observation in the RW (audit-script VALID_KINDS lacks 'part' kind) is real and worth tinker surfacing — 2 Part-N references at the surface (Part 5; Part 5, Test 1) remain bare specifically because the rewriter's audit-kind validator wouldn't accept Part-N preview_source_refs. Substituting these would close a further -2 in forward_references penalty. The rewriter already flagged this in rationale_phase2_notes; documenting in the review record so the next operator pass on PROP-041 sees the same signal from an independent reviewer. (2) Stylistic: the inserted 'X, which Y, for the full Z' construction is slightly heavy — readers will encounter the relative clause inside the 'See ... for ...' wrapper four times in close succession. The original surface used 'See X for Y' four times; the rewrite now expands all four to 'See X, which Z, for Y'. This is the safest preservation strategy (no original text reordered), but the section's prose density increases noticeably. Not a hole — just noting that Cat G's verbose form interacts with already-long surfaces in a way that future rewriter iterations may want to optimize (e.g., 'See Section 4.1's audit of the 28-scored... dashboard' as an alternative phrasing). NOT a blocker for THIS RW.
summary_for_decider: APPROVE RW-002. All applicable RWR checks pass (RWR-1,2,3,5,6,7,8,9,12). 0 major + 0 moderate + 0 minor. The 4 Category G forward-reference previews (Sec 2.9 x2, Sec 6.2, Sec 4.1) are well-grounded paraphrases of source section tldrs with no new claims, no dropped claims, no citation drift, no numerical drift, and verdict/argument structure preserved verbatim. Predicted composite delta +2.4 clears the 1.5 gate. Two non-blocking operator-attention items recorded in curmudgeon_open_concerns: (a) audit-script VALID_KINDS gap blocking Part-N substitutions; (b) stylistic heaviness of the chosen Cat G phrasing in already-long surfaces — neither alters the approval.
